name: DrawingPluginBase.InputDefinition
summary: The InputDefinition class is a class for defining the plug-in dependency over the input (points or identifiers). The user implemented method DefineInput() of the DrawingPluginBase interface should return an array list of input definition instances. This defines the points and identifiers the plug-in will receive as input when the Run() method is called.

constructors.name: DrawingPluginBase.InputDefinition(Identifier, List<Point>)
constructors.summary: Creates a new input definition with multiple points.

constructors.name: DrawingPluginBase.InputDefinition(Identifier, Point)
constructors.summary: Creates a new input definition instance with one point.

params.name: viewId
params.doc: The identifier for the view object.

constructors.name: DrawingPluginBase.InputDefinition(Identifier, Identifier)
constructors.summary: Creates a new input definition instance with one identifier.

params.name: viewId
params.doc: The identifier for the view object.

constructors.name: DrawingPluginBase.InputDefinition(Identifier, Point, Point)
constructors.signature: public InputDefinition(Identifier viewId, Point point1, Point point2)
constructors.summary: Creates a new input definition instance with two points.

params.name: viewId
params.doc: The identifier for the view object.

constructors.name: DrawingPluginBase.InputDefinition(Identifier, Point, Point, Point)
constructors.signature: public InputDefinition(Identifier viewId, Point point1, Point point2, Point point3)
constructors.summary: Creates a new input definition instance with three points.

params.name: viewId
params.doc: The identifier for the view object.

methods.name: GetInput
methods.summary: Returns the input Tekla Structures gave to the plug-in. The input is either a point instance, an identifier instance, an array list of points or an array list of identifiers. This is based on the input format returned from the DefineInput() method.

returns.doc: The input to use in the Run() method.

properties.name: Type
properties.summary: The type of the input the current instance contains.
